extra.reasoning: The decision system computes acuity from three priority-3 rules that each map a true input to emergent: severe_headache→emergent, fever→emergent, vomiting→emergent. The default sets acuity:=routine when no rule fires. Critically, dyspnea does not appear in any rule and therefore has zero influence on the output. For any fixed assignment of (severe_headache, fever, vomiting): if any is true, acuity=emergent regardless of dyspnea; if all are false, acuity=routine (default) regardless of dyspnea. Hence toggling dyspnea from false to true while holding the other three inputs constant leaves acuity exactly unchanged in every case. The monotonicity property requires acuity to be no lower after setting dyspnea true; equality satisfies "no lower." Since acuity never changes when dyspnea is toggled, it can never be lowered. The property holds over the entire 16-point input space.
